title: Vidveṣaṇa-karma
century: Medieval (c. 7th–14th c.); commentarial tradition to 18th c.
description: The fourth of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the discord-generation (*vidveṣaṇa*) rite for creating enmity, division, or separation between parties (typically hostile parties, or parties allied against the practitioner). High caution — the first of the "fierce four" ṣaṭ-karma (vidveṣaṇa, uccāṭana, māraṇa, plus the optional mohana). Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.
